rationale: The sole substantive ground-truth concern (yJader's inline comment) explains that dropping the already_submitted gate is correct because diffusion StagePool rejects list prompts in both submit_initial and submit_update. Candidate X independently verifies this exact fact (stage_pool.py:951, 1022) and reaches the identical conclusion, also confirming the DiffusionOutput fix against the real ZImagePipeline return type — grounded and correctly APPROVEs. Candidate Y flags the same line as a 'major' risk in the opposite direction ('verify submit_initial accepts a scalar; if it expects a list this will error'), never actually checks stage_pool.py, and lands on REQUEST CHANGES — contradicting the true (and verifiable) behavior, which tanks its precision despite superficially concrete file/line citations.
